title: Vue.js Custom Directives Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of low-level DOM access, directive lifecycle hooks, and simple v-focus use cases.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary purpose of a Custom Directive in Vue.js?
options: To provide reusable low-level DOM access and interactions directly on an element.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Which lifecycle hook should you use if you want to focus an element as soon as it the mounted into the DOM?
Which and/or what is the prefix for using a custom directive named 'focus' in your template?
Does a custom directive always have access to the actual DOM element (el)?
options: Yes, as the first argument in its lifecycle hooks.
No, it only has access to its value.
Only in older versions of Vue.

questions.question: Can you register a custom directive locally within a single component instead of globally for the whole app?
options: Yes, using the 'directives' option in your component.
No, all directives must be global.
